title: Coreopsis buchii
intro: Coreopsis buchii, commonly known as tickseed, is a flowering plant native to North America, belonging to the Asteraceae family. Traditionally used in herbal medicine, Coreopsis buchii is valued for its anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which can help alleviate symptoms of digestive issues, skin conditions, and respiratory problems. The bioactive constituents of Coreopsis buchii include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and sesquiterpenes, which contribute to its medicinal effects. Coreopsis buchii is commonly prepared as a tea, tincture, or infusion, and is also used in topical creams and salves to support skin health and wound healing.
taxonomy: Coreopsis buchii is a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae. It is commonly known as Buch's tickseed or Buch's coreopsis. The classification of Coreopsis buchii is as follows: it belongs to the genus Coreopsis, which is a group of annual and perennial plants native to the Americas. Coreopsis buchii is further divided into the tribe Coreopsideae, and the subfamily Asteroideae. Coreopsis buchii's classification is part of the larger classification system of flowering plants.
